source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Walter Oil & Gas Corporation Well #001

event.top_event: Flash fire from ignited gas during welding operations
actions_taken: Three injured employees sent to hospital
Gas detector sent down inside casing to determine gas presence at depth
event.summary: On May 17, 2014, at 0830 hours, while performing abandonment operations on Walter Oil & Gas's #001 well on the Rowan Gorilla IV, three employees were burned by a flash fire. The crew was welding pad eyes to the inside of a 36-inch casing to enable removal. Burning slag fell inside the wellbore, igniting gas that had migrated from the reservoir after the casing was cut. The three employees suffered first and second degree burns to their upper bodies. Investigation revealed gas was present at approximately 40 feet depth inside the casing but not at the surface where employees were working.
recommendations: Prior to performing any welding operations on casing, the operator should take measures to ensure that not only the immediate area is clear of all hydrocarbons, but that there is no gas present inside the casing as well.

threats.name: Gas migration into wellbore after casing cut

consequences.name: Flash fire and thermal burns to personnel

controls.name: Hot Work Permit
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify hazards and precautions before hot work
controls.lod_basis: Initial control to prevent hazardous work from proceeding without proper planning

supporting_text: A 'Hot Work Permit', along with a Job Safety Analysis (JSA), was filled out by the crew members before operations began. Although the documents were filled out as required, both failed to identify all of the hazards associated with the operation.

During the investigation, a Permit to Work was provided to the inspectors upon request but the form was incomplete. There are specific sections on the form that are provided to allow for the gas readings to be recorded and the certification dates of the equipment to be documented, but all were blank.

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify job hazards and risk assessment
controls.lod_basis: Initial hazard identification and risk assessment before work begins

supporting_text: The JSA completed prior to the start of the job failed to identify any hazards associated with performing welding operations in a hazardous area or the risk of gas being ignited from inside the well.

The employees failed to identify that the hazards and risk associated with the job changed when the operation had to be changed. The risk associated with the job should have been reevaluated when the crew realized they were going to have to weld the pad eyes to the inside of the casing, instead of being able to weld them to the outside of the casing as they had done previously.

controls.name: Gas detection and monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Engineering/administrative control to detect presence of flammable gas before hot work
controls.lod_basis: Detection of hazardous condition (gas presence) to prevent ignition source exposure

supporting_text: Employees failed to properly complete a 'Hot Work Permit' and take gas readings as required.

The investigation following the accident showed that the gas readings near the surface, where the injured employees were burned, had no gas present. A gas detector was then sent down the inside of the casing, and at a depth of approximately 40 feet, the gas readings increased significantly.
There are specific sections on the form that are provided to allow for the gas readings to be recorded and the certification dates of the equipment to be documented, but all were blank.

controls.name: Supervision of welding operations
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure compliance with welding procedures and safety protocols
controls.lod_basis: Supervisory oversight to prevent deviation from approved procedures and ensure hazard controls are implemented

supporting_text: Walter's approved welding plan calls for strict supervision of welding operations that take place outside of the 'Safe Welding Area'. More oversight on the part of the Supervisors may have helped prevent the accident.
several of the precautions stated in Walter's approved 'Welding, Burning, and Hot-Tapping Plan' were ignored or overlooked, increasing the chances of an accident.

controls.name: Welding, Burning, and Hot-Tapping Plan compliance
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure approved procedures are followed during hot work
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to prevent unsafe hot work practices

supporting_text: Failure of the crew to follow Walter's approved 'Welding, Burning, and Hot-Tapping Plan'
several of the precautions stated in Walter's approved 'Welding, Burning, and Hot-Tapping Plan' were ignored or overlooked, increasing the chances of an accident.

controls.name: Control of ignition sources (slag containment)
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent burning slag from entering wellbore
controls.lod_basis: Prevention of ignition source from contacting flammable gas

supporting_text: As the pad eyes were being welded to the inside of the pipe, burning slag was allowed to fall inside of the wellbore. The gas that was present inside the well was ignited, which caused flames to be shot out of the casing at the surface.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
